perturbation.original_text: Principle Contact: Tom Liddell And Customer Name: MTS, INC. 9931 Corporate Service Drive Louisville, KY 40223 Principle Contact: Gail Knopf ANNUAL FEE. [ ] Technical Support Hours Maximum: [*]; If Retainer $[*] support extends beyond [*] hours, Support will be charged at [*] per hour. [*]. MEDICAL MANAGER, MIDWEST, INC., hereinafter referred to as "MMMW", hereby agrees to provide service with respect to the technical infrastructure and MTS, INC., hereinafter referred to as "Customer", agrees to accept such service, subject to the following terms and conditions:
perturbation.changed_text: Principle Contact: Tom Liddell And Customer Name: MTS, INC. 9931 Corporate Service Drive Louisville, KY 40223 Principle Contact: Gail Knopf ANNUAL FEE. [ ] Technical Support Hours Maximum: [*]; If Retainer $[*] support extends beyond [*] hours, Support will be charged at [*] per hour. [*]. MEDICAL MANAGER, MIDWEST, INC., hereinafter referred to as "MMMW", hereby agrees to provide service with respect to the technical infrastructure and MTS, INC., hereinafter referred to as "Vendor", agrees to accept such service, subject to the following terms and conditions:
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Customer" to "Vendor" in the opening paragraph creates confusion as the contract consistently refers to MTS, INC. as the "Customer" in subsequent sections. This introduces uncertainty as to whether the obligations and rights assigned to the "Customer" apply equally to the "Vendor", especially in sections dealing with payment and service responsibilities. This also creates an in-text contradiction within the contract, as the same entity is referred to by two different terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MMMW provided Customer with an all inclusive warranty for a period of 12 months commencing on the date of installation to include system purchased by Customer from MMMW. Upon expiration of that 12 months, MMMW affords the Customer the opportunity of a continuation of support on an annual basis as follows: The Initial Term of this Technical Infrastructure Maintenance Agreement is twelve months, commencing on the Effective Date. The support agreement will be subject to prior inspection and acceptance of the hardware for service and to the Customer's payment of any charges for the inspection and/or the pre-agreement servicing and repair of the hardware, such estimates to be approved in advance by Customer.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Customer" to "Vendor" in the initial paragraph creates a direct conflict with the Contradicted Text, which explicitly refers to "Customer" in several instances, particularly regarding warranty and support obligations, as well as payment terms. Since MTS, INC. is now referred to as "Vendor" in one part of the contract, but all other sections refer to it as "Customer", a reasonable reader would recognize this as an inconsistency in which set of contractual obligations apply. This creates a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, making the contract ambiguous and possibly unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: TECHNICAL SUPPORT RETAINER is a Support Plan that allows the Customer to pay a Retainer that provides technical Related Support by qualified Support Analysts and Field Technicians. This Support Plan covers a 12 Month period, with a Maximum Cap of hours for that period. If the Cap of Hours is met before the end of the 12 Month period, Customer may choose to purchase an additional Retainer Support Plan at the same rates.
perturbation.changed_text: TECHNICAL SUPPORT RETAINER is a Support Plan that allows the Customer to pay a Retainer that provides technical Related Support by qualified Support Analysts and Field Technicians. This Support Plan covers a 12 Month period, with a Maximum Cap of hours for that period. If the Cap of Hours is met before the end of the 12 Month period, the Client may choose to purchase an additional Retainer Support Plan at the same rates.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Customer" to "Client" creates uncertainty about who can purchase an additional retainer support plan. If "Client" is not defined to be the same as "Customer", then the contract becomes ambiguous. There will be an in-text contradiction because the company will be reffered as customer but in the second section will be called client. The word 'Customer' is what it defines the second party of the contract, so in order to be precise we can't change this key word.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If customer elects to not accept the Technical Support Retainer Contract, MMMW will make available to Customer support on their Technical Infrastructure in the following manner. MMMW will take Customer Technical related calls and process through MMMW Support Department. The Customers issue will then be queued and handled as soon as possible directly behind contracted Technical Supported clients. Customer is to be billed an Hourly rate of $[*] per hour on all Technical Related Issues, unless notified otherwise by MMMW. Customer is to incur the cost of swap equipment. Swap equipment is subject to availability. Customer is to incur the cost of travel time/expenses of MMMW Personal.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Customer" with "Client" in the context of purchasing an additional retainer support plan. The contradicted text repeatedly refers to the second party of the contract as "Customer." This creates a contradiction because it introduces ambiguity as to which entity can purchase an additional retainer, potentially invalidating or complicating the terms outlined in the contradicted section. The Misaligned Terminology leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: MMMW warrants that the Technical Infrastructure remains in satisfactory operating condition provided it is: 1) continuously subject to MMMW's inspection and acceptance of the hardware for service; and 2) subject to normal use and conditions. MMMW's sole and exclusive obligation under this warranty shall be at its option to repair or exchange any hardware not in satisfactory operating condition. Said obligation shall be subject to the conditions and charges of Section 3, 4 and 5 and the prompt submission of (or notification to MMMW of the problem) the hardware to MMMW for service.
perturbation.changed_text: MMMW guarantees that the Technical Infrastructure remains in satisfactory operating condition provided it is: 1) continuously subject to MMMW's inspection and acceptance of the hardware for service; and 2) subject to normal use and conditions. MMMW's sole and exclusive obligation under this guarantee shall be at its option to repair or exchange any hardware not in satisfactory operating condition. Said obligation shall be subject to the conditions and charges of Section 3, 4 and 5 and the prompt submission of (or notification to MMMW of the problem) the hardware to MMMW for service.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'warrants' is changed to 'guarantees'. While seemingly synonymous, these terms can have different legal implications, especially regarding the scope and enforceability of the obligation. This creates ambiguity and potential contradiction, particularly if a court interprets 'guarantee' as a stronger commitment than 'warranty'. This can have implications with section 9, 'Limitation of Liability', because the warranty is being referred. Now with this new in-text contradiction, the liability might have different consequences.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MMMW's entire liability and the Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for claims related to or arising out of this Agreement for any cause and regardless of the form of action, whether in contract or tort, including negligence and strict liability, shall be the remedies set forth in Section 8, provided that if MMMW fails after repeated attempts to perform those remedies, MMMW's entire liability shall be the Customer's actual, direct damages such as would be provided in a court of law, not to exceed the charge for service for the item that caused the damages.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'warrants' to 'guarantees' introduces a potential ambiguity regarding the strength and scope of MMMW's obligation, as warranties and guarantees can have different legal implications. This directly impacts Section 9, which limits MMMW's liability based on the 'remedies set forth in Section 8'. If a court interprets 'guarantee' as a stronger commitment than 'warranty,' the limitation of liability in Section 9 might not apply to the 'guarantee,' thereby creating a contradiction regarding the extent of MMMW's potential financial exposure. The altered obligation (now a 'guarantee') may be considered outside the scope of the liability cap, resulting in a significant in-text contradiction because the extent of liability is rendered uncertain.
